Off Calendar Jan-21-2022 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. NICOLE VALES, Esq. of Schroeder Loscotoff
Atty. ALICE J. PARK, Esq. of Guslani Simonson & Clause LLP (Tel: 650-637-9100) for defendant/cross-complainant/cross-defendant Exxact Corporation;
Atty. BRIDGET A. MCKINSTRY, Esq. of Law Offices of Thomas J. Burns (Tel: 415-227-2300) for cross-defendant Levi's Plaza T.I.C..

HEARING RE: CROSS-DEFENDANT LEVI'S PLAZA T.I.C.'S DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

"Cross-defendant Levi's Plaza T.I.C.'s demurrer to the cross-complaint of defendant/cross-complainant Exxact Corporation" is overruled. It is argued that the cross-complaint repeats affirmative defenses from Exxact's answer, but California's liberal pleadings rules permit this. (Paragon Real Estate Group of San Francisco, Inc. v. Hansen (2009) 178 Cal.App.4th 177, 185-86.) The opinions Levi's Plaza cites are from other jurisdictions or inapposite scenarios.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Cross Complaint
Off Calendar Jun-30-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. WILLIAM LOSCOTOFF, Esq. of Schroeder Loscotoff Stevens LLP (Tel: 916-438-8300) for plaintiff;
Atty. VICTOR C. NG, Esq. of Hayes Scott Bonino Ellingson Guslani Simonson & Clause LLP (Tel: 650-637-9100) for defendant Exxact Corporation.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT EXXACT CORPORATION's Motion For Leave To File Cross Complaint.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant Exxact Corporation's motion for leave to file cross-complaint is granted. (Code Civ. Proc. secs. 428.10, 428.50(c).) Plaintiff Zurich American Insurance Company does not show that it will be prejudiced or that Exxact is acting in bad faith. Contrary to Zurich's unsupported argument, Exxact is not required to submit admissible evidence to support its motion. If Zurich wishes to challenge the viability of the proposed declaratory relief cause of action, it may file a demurrer or other appropriate motion. (See California Casualty Gen. Ins. v. Superior Court (1985) 173 Cal.App.3d 274, 281 ["the preferable practice would be to permit the amendment and allow the parties to test its legal sufficiency by demurrer, motion for judgment on the pleadings or other appropriate proceedings."].)

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Eric Throne, CSR No. 7855, Email: eric.throne.csr@gmail.com, Tel: 707-305-8964 (302/EPS)
